resources.string: 8 AI-enhanced accessibility rules — each violated on its own screen.
Image carries informational text, but the alt label is missing or generic.
Non-decorative ImageView elements must have a clear contentDescription. Violations: missing, empty, inadequate (generic/filename/mismatched), or hidden from screen readers.

Every interactive element needs a non-null, descriptive label. Covers interactable (ImageButton/clickable View), Switch, Checkbox, and Editable (EditText) elements. Violations: missing, generic, or contextually inaccurate label.
Screen-reader focus order does not match the logical reading order.
Visual arrangement contradicts the natural left-to-right / top-to-bottom flow.
Section titles look like headings visually but are not exposed as headings to TalkBack — heading navigation skips them.
Body copy, ads, or error states are marked as headings — heading navigation lands on noise instead of real sections.
Links labelled \"click here\", \"read more\", or a raw URL don\'t convey their destination out of context.
